prompt: Event CG illustration for a kinetic novel, full-scene composition, 16:9, art-book / key-visual quality. Confident expressive anime lineart, refined soft cel shading with subtle gradient rendering, painterly detailed background, delicate cinematic lighting, muted earthy palette with restrained accent saturation. Appealing and distinctive, not generic. No text, no speech bubbles, no watermark. Use the character(s) shown in the attached reference image(s), keeping their faces, hair, colors, markings and outfits consistent; IGNORE the flat magenta backgrounds of the references. Capture attempt on a narrow forest road walled by dark trees, late day: two dark-armored slavetakers (rank-and-file fighters in the armor style of the second reference, NOT his face) wrestling to SEIZE Avram: the young man from the first reference — dense dark almost-black curly ringlet hair in a short modern cut, clean-shaven youthful face, green-brown hooded traveler's cloak over tan leather armor with a plain roughspun linen undershirt, and worn leather boots — ALIVE and UNHARMED: one has both hands clamped on Avram's sword arm dragging it down, the other is throwing a loop of rope toward him or grabbing his cloak from behind. ABSOLUTELY NO raised swords, NO killing strokes, NO blades pointed at Avram — their own weapons are sheathed or lowered; this is a GRAPPLE to take a valuable prisoner, like guards subduing someone, not an attempt to hurt him. Avram straining against the hold, teeth set, still gripping his sword. Crowded, physical, close-quarters composition, motion energy. No blood, no wounds. CRITICAL: EXACTLY ONE Avram in the frame — NO duplicate, NO twin. ABSOLUTELY CRITICAL: Avram is CLEAN-SHAVEN — NO beard, NO stubble; a YOUNG man in his late teens with a smooth youthful face exactly as in the first reference.
_note: prompt above is the FINAL (3rd-try) version that produced the shipped asset: v1 gave Avram stubble/age-up, v2 had a slavetaker raising a killing-stroke sword (defeats the capture-not-kill point). Final = pure grapple + rope, weapons lowered.

prompt: Event CG illustration for a kinetic novel, full-scene composition, 16:9, art-book / key-visual quality. Confident expressive anime lineart, refined soft cel shading with subtle gradient rendering, painterly detailed background, delicate cinematic lighting, muted earthy palette with restrained accent saturation. Appealing and distinctive, not generic. No text, no speech bubbles, no watermark. Use the character(s) shown in the attached reference image(s), keeping their faces, hair, colors, markings and outfits consistent; IGNORE the flat magenta backgrounds of the references. The first kill, on a narrow forest road walled by dark trees, late day: Avram: the young man from the first reference — dense dark almost-black curly ringlet hair in a short modern cut, clean-shaven youthful face, green-brown hooded traveler's cloak over tan leather armor with a plain roughspun linen undershirt, and worn leather boots — has run one dark-armored slavetaker (rank-and-file fighter in the armor style of the second reference, NOT his face) through: Avram's two hands still grip the hilt of his sword, and that SAME single blade passes INTO the man's chest, with its point emerging from the man's upper back. ONE continuous sword: hilt in Avram's hands, point out the man's back — the geometry must connect. The man is folding backward over the blade, mid-collapse, head lolling. REALISTIC blood: blood on the emerging point of the blade, a dark red stain spreading through the man's gambeson around the entry wound, a few drops falling to the dirt. The amount a real sword wound would produce — grounded, NOT exaggerated: no spray, no fountain, no spatter across the scene. Avram's face is set and distant — no triumph, no rage. Dynamic diagonal composition. CRITICAL: there is EXACTLY ONE sword in the entire image, the one impaling the man, held by Avram — NO second sword anywhere, NO sword lying on the ground; EXACTLY ONE Avram — NO duplicate. ABSOLUTELY CRITICAL: Avram is CLEAN-SHAVEN — NO beard, NO stubble; a YOUNG man with a smooth youthful face exactly as in the first reference.
_note: FINAL shipped prompt (blood pass, author-directed 2026-07-04: realistic blood, don't sanitize). History: v1-v2 were the sanitized 'NO gore' versions (v2 clean version archived at claude-notes/raw/cg_first_kill_clean.png); v3 blood pass had a two-swords artifact; final adds the ONE-continuous-sword geometry constraint. One 'no image in response' API hiccup, succeeded on internal retry.

prompt: Event CG illustration for a kinetic novel, full-scene composition, 16:9, art-book / key-visual quality. Confident expressive anime lineart, refined soft cel shading with subtle gradient rendering, painterly detailed background, delicate cinematic lighting, muted earthy palette with restrained accent saturation. Appealing and distinctive, not generic. No text, no speech bubbles, no watermark. Use the character(s) shown in the attached reference image(s), keeping their faces, hair, colors, markings and outfits consistent; IGNORE the flat magenta backgrounds of the references. Immediately after the strike, ground-level camera on a narrow forest road, cold late-day light: Haurvatat, the wolf-beastfolk woman from the first reference — wolf ears, two-tone russet-brown-and-white hair and fur (a white streak through the fringe, white insides of the ears, a white-tipped tail), amber eyes, freckles across the nose, a small notch in one ear, clawed hands with dark nails, and a solid seamless dark blackened-iron slave collar engraved with dim NON-glowing runes, in full-coverage steel-and-leather fighting armor — collapsed on her side on the dirt road in front of Avram: the young man from the second reference — dense dark almost-black curly ringlet hair in a short modern cut, clean-shaven youthful face, green-brown hooded traveler's cloak over tan leather armor with a plain roughspun linen undershirt, and worn leather boots. Her sword fallen near her open hand, eyes half-open and unfocused. She has just taken a dark magic lance through the breastplate: a punched, scorched hole in the center of her chest armor, and REALISTIC blood from the wound — dark red blood welling from the hole, running down the curve of the breastplate, and beginning to soak into the dirt beneath her; a thin line of blood at the corner of her mouth. The amount of blood a serious chest wound would actually produce — grounded and realistic, NOT exaggerated: no spatter on the trees, no huge pool, no glistening horror-style excess. Avram dropping to his knees, reaching toward her. CRITICAL: EXACTLY ONE Avram and EXACTLY ONE Haurvatat in the frame — no duplicates of either.
_note: FINAL shipped prompt (blood pass, author-directed 2026-07-04: realistic blood, don't sanitize). The sanitized first version is archived at claude-notes/raw/cg_her_fallen_clean.png. Shipped image has a small smoky silhouetted figure far down the road (not prompted; reads as a distant slavetaker) — flagged to author.
